result: I write about the small rituals that tether us to mornings. Steam lifting from a mug, a spoon clink, sunlight through blinds that turns dust into confetti. I describe the way a city breathes when the first train rolls, and how a cat stretches with a confidence I wish I could learn. I talk about curiosity in the quiet: the way we read a note left on a fridge magnet, how a neighbor
